translated: [CORNER: Song Watchalong] Now then, let's take a look at the 3DMV right away.
Here it is.
Did you see that expression just now?
They were smiling.
It's so good.
Wow, that was wonderful.
Honestly, I was blown away when I first saw the MV for "Consilium."
I was like, "I can't believe the scenery changes this much!"
Like, it goes from a school-like setting to an after-school vibe, with shots through windows showing leaves...
And when they're walking down the hallway, you see those little things floating in the light.
No, it's not Wi-Fi signals.
What would you call it?
Like, not exactly dust, but you can see little particles in the air.
The direction is so detailed, I kept wondering how they even did that.
It was really, really amazing.
And the way everyone spins around and the characters swap out... the direction was just incredible.
I loved it.
It's great that we can look back at it like this.
It's so good, and the 2DMV is also fantastic, so I want you all to see that too.
Regarding the 2DMV...
Well, this is just my personal take,
and since it hasn't been officially stated, I'm not entirely sure, but...
In the 2DMV, Mizuki is sitting at a table with a cake, like a birthday seat,
and the camera pans across things that symbolize Mizuki.
You see things like the letter from their sister, clothes,
ribbons, and stuff like that appearing.
The 2D version was really lovely too.
Both were amazing.
I was surprised by how much information was packed in there.
Also, about the song... I feel like Mizuki's songs usually have a lot of painful lyrics.
I'm not trying to compare it to past songs, but I felt this one had a lot of very positive lyrics.
Even so, I still feel like Mizuki hasn't fully opened up yet, and there are still swirling emotions inside.
So, I consulted with the director about the nuances, like how much weakness
or strength we should add or subtract. We talked a lot while creating this song.
For the parts that sound like a radio broadcast,
we discussed whether to put a lot of emotion into it or try making the voice a bit raspy.
We consulted on many of those subtle nuances for this track.
I hope you enjoy both the full 2DMV and the 3DMV where Mizuki smiles at you.
Both are already uploaded to the official ProSeka YouTube channel,
so please go check them out.
Well then, although it was a short time,
this concludes the ProSeka AfterTalk for the "Reaching Out to an Unending Tomorrow" edition.
To everyone watching the stream, how did you like it?
Let me take a look at the comments for a bit.
Hmm.
"The atmosphere of Oonuma Parsley-san's MV and Niigo's 2D version were different, but both were great."
That's so true.
For Mizuki, how should I put it?
Well, students have their career paths, and ProSeka is about to reach its 5th anniversary.
Mizuki has been through a lot leading up to the 5th anniversary, but they're looking forward now.
It shows that this happened, which allowed them to look forward,
and that feeling of "I'm going to do my best" is summarized there.
I think the Niigo version feels like it's decorated with those feelings.
As for Oonuma-san's version, how should I describe it?
It's like an MV where a tired working adult is encouraging themselves to keep going and look ahead.
I felt that "Consilium" is a song with a very wide range depending on the singer and how it's expressed.
So please, check out Oonuma-san's... would you call it a 2D version?
What would you call that?
The music video?
I hope you enjoy that MV as well.
Looking back at the event story, Niigo stories have often been heavy or focused on suffocating situations.
But I think this story is one that high schoolers—from first to third years—
can really relate to on a personal level.
It's an event story that feels very true to life.
Whether it's deciding on a career path, or lacking confidence in your own interests... if there's anyone who feels they can't love what they like right now,
and that goes for working adults too, not just students... if you haven't seen the story or heard the song yet,
I think you'll find words that resonate with you exactly because of who you are right now.
So, I hope everyone, both adults and students, enjoys it to the very end.
If you haven't finished the story for "Reaching Out to an Unending Tomorrow," you can read it all from the event story archive once the next event starts.
It will probably be uploaded to YouTube at a later date as well,
so it's perfectly fine to wait for that.
[CORNER: Fan Letter Corner] AfterTalk is always looking for your messages.
You can submit them through the AfterTalk submission channel on the official ProSeka Discord,
or via email.
Please send them in once the next AfterTalk event begins.
We look forward to your messages regarding the event.
Well, August is almost over, and once September passes, the Thanksgiving festival awaits.
We've been busy making preparations here and there,
so I'd be happy if you look forward to that too.
August is ending, but...
